title: Atropis phryganodes
taxonomy: Atropis phryganodes is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Apiaceae. It is native to the Himalayan region and is also known as Himalayan parsley. The plant is a perennial herb with a number of medicinal properties. Atropis phryganodes is closely related to Atropis linophylloides and is often found in mountainous regions. Further classification of the plant is also provided as Atropis phryganodes (syn. Peucedanum phryganodes), however more information on this plant is needed to confirm this classification.
uses: Atropis phryganodes is a plant species known for its potential medicinal uses, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy.
